ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for crayfish hor fun; mapped to the closest parent class 'rice noodle dish' (FOODON_03315595) as an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice noodles, cooked — used as the primary carbohydrate base anchor for this composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite code for crayfish hor fun; A00GJ (cooked rice noodles) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the dominant ingredient.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for crayfish hor fun or equivalent composite rice noodle dish with seafood.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of crayfish hor fun as served at a Singapore hawker centre or food court typically weighs approximately 350 g, comprising rice noodles, crayfish, mushroom, chicken, and soya gravy.

nutrient_source.title: Crayfish Hor Fun — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Crayfish Hor Fun (cr_id F05060034). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Iron was reported as -1 (not available) in the source record.

gi_source.title: Rice noodles, cooked — GI value 61 (analogous estimate); adjusted to 56 for composite dish with protein and fibre moderating effect
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for crayfish hor fun. The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 61 for plain cooked rice noodles. The composite dish includes protein from crayfish and chicken (2.13 g/100g) and dietary fibre (1.3 g/100g) in a soya gravy matrix, which moderates glycaemic response. A conservative downward adjustment to GI 56 (medium category) is applied, consistent with published evidence that protein and fibre co-ingestion reduces postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 42.91) / 100 ≈ 24.0; rounded to 25.7 accounting for 350 g serving with 12.26 g carbohydrate per 100 g (total carbohydrate = 42.91 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 257 mg per 100 g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 900 mg sodium, representing 39–45% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce to keep single-meal sodium below 600 mg; pairing with potassium-rich vegetables such as spinach or broccoli can partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 12.26 g per 100 g from refined rice noodles; estimated GI 56 (medium) yields a glycemic load of approximately 24 per standard 350 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the serving to 150–200 g to keep the glycemic load below 15, choose a smaller noodle portion with extra crayfish and mushroom for protein and fibre, and avoid adding extra gravy which increases both sodium and refined starch intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 257 mg per 100 g and estimated saturated fat of 0.7 g per 100 g; the dish is stir-fried rather than deep-fried, limiting total fat to 3.1 g per 100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, portion should be restricted to 150–200 g to limit sodium and saturated fat intake; crayfish provides lean protein and some omega-3 fatty acids which are cardioprotective, but the soya gravy should be requested light to reduce sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crayfish Hor Fun (龙虾河粉) is a popular Singaporean Chinese hawker dish consisting of broad, flat rice noodles (hor fun) stir-fried and served in a rich, silky soya-based gravy together with whole crayfish in shell, sliced black mushrooms, and shredded chicken meat. The dish is a variant of the classic 'wat tan hor' style, where the gravy is thickened with egg and cornstarch to create a smooth, glossy coating over the noodles. The texture is soft and slippery from the rice noodles, with a savoury umami depth from soy sauce and oyster sauce, and a mild sweetness from the crayfish. Per 100 g, the dish provides 85 kcal, 2.13 g protein, 3.1 g fat, and 12.26 g carbohydrate, with moderate sodium at 257 mg. The estimated glycemic index is 56 (medium), making portion control important for glycaemic management. Crayfish contributes lean protein and trace omega-3 fatty acids, while black mushrooms add dietary fibre (1.3 g per 100 g) and antioxidants. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the presence of crayfish and chicken, and contains soy and gluten allergens from the sauces. It is commonly served at Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and school canteens as a lunch or dinner option. Clinically, the primary concern is sodium content; a standard 350 g plate delivers approximately 900 mg sodium, requiring portion control for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05060034) at confidence 0.95. The dish is an exact match in the primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database applied to the closest approved analogue (cooked rice noodles), adjusted for protein and fibre content of the composite dish.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the HPB Foodo source record (F05060034) reports iron as -1 (data not available), indicating this micronutrient was not measured or reported for this specific dish preparation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite hawker dish are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 0.7 g per 100 g based on the fat profile of a comparable stir-fried rice noodle dish with mixed protein (crayfish and chicken) from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. Cultural tags in the input record incorrectly listed 'western' as primary cuisine and included 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' dietary tags; these have been corrected to 'Singaporean Chinese' cuisine and the vegetarian/plant-based flags removed, as the dish contains crayfish and chicken. Halal status is uncertain as crayfish (a crustacean) is not universally accepted as halal under all Islamic jurisprudence schools; is_halal has been set to false pending formal halal certification confirmation. The input GI value of 0 was unsupported and has been replaced with an analogous estimate of 56 from the University of Sydney GI Database.
